the surrounding district.


While here the chamber will bring
before his attention the necessity of
the construction of canal from the
Pend dOreille river. near Newport.
to divert the waters of this river to
the headwaters of the Little Spokane
river so as to increase the volume
of water In the main Spokane through
the Big Bend country. This problem
Is one that In importance and the
benefits to be derived should be
handled by the national government


The building of such a canal would
give to the farmers of the Big Bend
sufficient water at all seasons of the
year to raise alfalfa. garden truck
and fruits. At present during the
summer the waters of the Spokane
through that section are too low to
permit of any systematic attempt to
bring same onto the adjoining lands
